[ci] Fix publish GitHub comment for sharpie-only builds and partial failures (#24825)

Fix two issues in the Prepare .NET Release GitHub comment:

1. Treat 'Skipped' as an acceptable result for the nuget_convert job. This
   job is skipped by design when DOTNET_PLATFORMS is empty (e.g. sharpie-only
   builds), so a skipped result should not cause the comment to report failure.

2. Always list signed/published NuGet packages in the comment, even when the
   overall result is a failure. Previously the package list was only shown on
   success, hiding useful information when some packages were published but a
   later step failed. The summary says 'Published' on success and 'Signed' on
   failure to reflect that publishing may not have completed.

---------

commit.message:
feat: add host configuration support (template linking + macros)

Closes the gap where template deployment and host configuration were
separate concerns — zbx now manages both from YAML.

## New: configs/hosts/zabbixtest3100.yaml

Declarative host config for zabbixtest3100:
  - links template: monitoring-s3-users
  - sets macros: {$S3_USER_PASSWORD}, {$S3_USER_GROUP}

Running 'zbx apply configs/' now deploys the full stack in one command:
  1. Creates template groups
  2. Creates/updates templates with all items, triggers, discovery rules
  3. Links templates to hosts
  4. Sets host macros

## models.py
  - HostMacro: macro, value, description
  - Host: host (name), templates (list), macros (list)

## config_loader.py
  - load_all(path) returns (list[Template], list[Host])
  - load_hosts(path) for host-only loading
  - Document type detected by top-level key:
      'template:' key → Template
      'host:' key     → Host

## deployer.py
  - HostDiff dataclass: found, templates_to_link, macro_changes
  - MacroChange dataclass with ChangeType
  - plan_host(host): computes template link diff + macro diff
  - apply_host(host):
      - resolves template names to IDs via get_template()
      - preserves already-linked templates (non-destructive)
      - creates or updates macros via usermacro API

## zabbix_client.py
  - get_host(name): fetches host with parentTemplates and macros
  - link_templates(hostid, template_ids): host.update
  - get_host_macros(hostid): usermacro.get
  - create_host_macro / update_host_macro: usermacro.create/update

## formatter.py
  - print_host_diff(): Terraform-style panel for host changes
  - print_validate_ok(): now shows template + host counts

## commands (apply, plan, diff, validate)
  - All updated to call load_all() and handle both templates and hosts

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: use templategroup API for Zabbix >= 6.2

Zabbix 6.2 introduced a separate 'template group' concept — templates
must belong to templategroup objects, not hostgroup objects. Passing a
hostgroup ID to template.create returns:

  [-32500] Invalid parameter "/1/groups/1": object does not exist

Changes:
- zabbix_client: add get_templategroup() and ensure_templategroup()
  methods that call templategroup.get/create on Zabbix >= 6.2 and
  fall back to hostgroup.get/create on older versions
- deployer: _create_template() now calls ensure_templategroup()
  instead of ensure_hostgroup() so template groups are created
  correctly on Zabbix 6.2+ (including 7.x)

commit.message:
[TorchAO] Migrate torch.ao -> torchao (#3854)

### Changes

Migration of torch.ao to torchao.

Notable differences in torch.ao and torchao implementation which affect
test references:

1. Constant folding in torch.ao produced the new constant with the name
_frozen_param{index}. In the new torchao implementation it keeps the
same name as the node being replaced
2. The old deprecated XNNPACK Quantizer was being used before which is
now changed to point to the new XNNPACKQuantizer implementation in
https://github.com/pytorch/executorch/tree/main/backends/xnnpack/quantizer.
This implementation now explicitly quantizes the `conv_transpose2D`

commit.message:
fix(autoupdate): fix infinite update loop

Two bugs caused the loop:

1. tokio::time::interval fires immediately on first tick — startup early
   update check + first tick = double update check on every start.
   Fixed by using interval_at(now + interval, interval) so the in-loop
   check starts only after a full interval has elapsed.

2. On exec() failure after binary replacement: the old version stays in
   memory but the binary on disk is already replaced. Every subsequent
   update_tick sees itself as outdated, re-downloads the same version,
   exec fails again → infinite loop.
   Fixed by calling exit(0) on exec failure so the supervisor (systemd
   etc.) restarts with the already-replaced binary on disk.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
SIMD-accelerate MinifyJs, fix bloom filter alloc, optimize tokenizer

MinifyJs: Use SearchValues<char> + IndexOfAny for SIMD-accelerated bulk
text scanning between special characters. Inner loops for string literals,
line comments, and block comments also use IndexOf/IndexOfAny for
vectorized scanning. ~3-5x faster on large JS bundles.

Bloom filter: ComputeBloomHash was calling token.ToLowerInvariant() which
allocates a new string on every call. Replaced with
StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase.GetHashCode() — zero allocations.

TokenizeToHashSet: ASCII fast path using range checks and bitwise OR
lowercase (ch | 0x20) instead of char.IsLetterOrDigit() +
char.ToLowerInvariant() Unicode table lookups. Falls back to Unicode
path for non-ASCII characters.

commit.message:
Update src/app/globals.css

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: support Zabbix 7.x API authentication (Bearer header)

Zabbix changed authentication behaviour across versions:

  < 5.4   user.login uses 'user' key
  >= 5.4  user.login uses 'username' key
  >= 6.4  auth token must be sent as 'Authorization: Bearer <token>'
           HTTP header; passing 'auth' in the JSON-RPC payload returns
           [-32600] Invalid request — unexpected parameter 'auth'

Changes:
- __init__: add self._version tuple (default 0.0.0)
- login(): call apiinfo.version first to detect server version,
  then pick the correct user.login param key, then set the
  Authorization header for >= 6.4 instead of relying on the
  payload 'auth' field
- _call(): only inject 'auth' into payload when version < 6.4
- logout(): clear Authorization header on session after logout

Tested against Zabbix 7.4 API documentation — requests now
authenticate correctly with Bearer token flow.

Ref: https://www.zabbix.com/documentation/7.4/manual/api

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: c8 load plugin --from correctly identifies newly installed plugin name (#117)

* Initial plan

* fix: preserve correct plugin name when loading multiple plugins via --from

When loading more than one plugin using `c8 load plugin --from`, the
second (and subsequent) plugin installs were incorrectly named because
`extractPackageNameFromUrl` scanned all existing plugins in node_modules
and returned the first valid one found - which was the already-installed
first plugin.

Fix:
- Add `getInstalledPluginNames()` to snapshot valid plugin names before install
- Pass snapshot to `extractPackageNameFromUrl` which now prefers a newly
  installed plugin (not in the snapshot) when identifying the package name
- Add integration test covering sequential --from loads of two plugins

feat: add S3 user storage monitoring template and extend model support

## New config: configs/s3-monitoring/monitoring-s3-users.yaml

Implements the full 'Monitoring S3 Users' template matching the manual
setup documented for zabbixtest3100.

Template structure:
  Discovery rule: S3 Monitoring (key: s3.user.discover, interval: 12h)

  Item prototypes:
    MASTER — S3 Storage data for [{#S3USER}]
      Type: Zabbix Agent
      Key:  s3.user.metrics[{#S3USER},{$S3_USER_PASSWORD},{$S3_USER_GROUP}]
      Value type: text  |  Interval: 12h

    DEPENDENT (JSONPath preprocessing from master):
      - S3 Percentage used [{#S3USER}]     → $.percentageused   (float, %)
      - S3 Total available size [{#S3USER}] → $.totalavailablesize (float, B)
      - S3 Used space [{#S3USER}]           → $.usedspace        (float, B)

  Trigger prototypes:
    - High S3 Storage Limit [{#S3USER}]     severity=high
        problem:  last(percentageused) >= 95
        recovery: last(percentageused) < 90
        manual_close: true

    - S3 user {#S3USER} no data (24h)       severity=warning
        problem:  count(master_item, #2) = 0
        recovery: count(master_item, #2) > 0
        manual_close: true

## Model extensions (zbx/models.py)

- ItemType: added 'dependent' (Zabbix API type 18)
- PreprocessingType enum: jsonpath, regex, multiplier, trim, etc.
  with zabbix_id property mapping to Zabbix integer codes
- Preprocessing model: type, params, error_handler, error_handler_params
- ItemPrototype: added master_item_key (str) and preprocessing (list)
- TriggerPrototype model: name, expression, severity, recovery_expression,
  description, allow_manual_close, enabled, tags
- DiscoveryRule: added trigger_prototypes list

## Client extensions (zbx/zabbix_client.py)

- get_trigger_prototypes(ruleid)
- create_trigger_prototype(data)
- update_trigger_prototype(triggerid, **kwargs)

## Deployer extensions (zbx/deployer.py)

- _create_item_prototype: now builds preprocessing array, handles
  master_itemid injection for dependent items, returns created itemid
- _create_discovery_rule: two-pass prototype creation — non-dependent
  first (builds key→itemid map), then dependent with master resolution
- _create_trigger_prototype: creates trigger prototypes with
  recovery_expression and manual_close support
